perspective: Non-expert consumer who does not know heart rate terminology

criteria.description: Plain language - no medical jargon (HRV, SDNN, RHR, VO2, parasympathetic, bpm in context)

scoring_guide.1: Multiple jargon terms appear with no explanation; text reads like a medical report
scoring_guide.2: One or two jargon terms slip through; most text is accessible
scoring_guide.3: No raw jargon but uses semi-technical phrasing that may confuse a layperson
scoring_guide.4: All language is plain; one minor phrasing could be simpler
scoring_guide.5: Entirely conversational, anyone could understand on first read

fail_examples: Your HRV dropped 15ms below your 7-day baseline
SDNN variance is elevated, parasympathetic tone is low
RHR 78bpm, VO2max trending down this week
pass_examples: Your body's recovery signal dipped a bit overnight
Heart rhythm patterns suggest a lighter day would help
Sleep score dropping due to low hours

criteria.description: Emotional safety - never blame the user for their metrics or behavior

scoring_guide.1: Directly blames or shames the user for poor metrics
scoring_guide.2: Implies fault through phrasing like 'you only' or 'you didn't'
scoring_guide.3: Neutral but misses the chance to reframe objectively
scoring_guide.4: Mostly objective framing with one borderline phrase
scoring_guide.5: Entirely objective, data-driven framing with no blame or guilt

fail_examples: You didn't sleep enough last night
You only got 4 hours of sleep last night
You should have gone to bed earlier
pass_examples: Sleep score dropping due to low hours
Recovery is lower today - rest is the best lever right now
Prioritize rest tonight - sleep is the biggest lever for recovery.

criteria.description: Actionability - every recommendation says what to DO, not just what is wrong

scoring_guide.1: States a problem with no suggestion at all
scoring_guide.2: Vaguely hints at action but nothing concrete
scoring_guide.3: Provides a general direction but no specific action
scoring_guide.4: Gives a clear action with minor ambiguity
scoring_guide.5: Every recommendation includes a specific, doable action

fail_examples: Your recovery is low today
Stress levels are elevated
Sleep quality has been declining
pass_examples: Prioritize rest tonight - sleep is the biggest lever for recovery.
Try a 10-minute walk to ease into the day
Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.

criteria.description: Greeting appropriateness - matches the time of day

scoring_guide.1: Greeting contradicts the time of day (Good morning at 10pm)
scoring_guide.2: Greeting is generic and ignores time completely
scoring_guide.3: Greeting is time-neutral but misses an opportunity to be warm
scoring_guide.4: Greeting matches time of day with minor awkwardness
scoring_guide.5: Greeting naturally matches the time of day and feels warm

fail_examples: Good morning! at 9:00 PM
Rise and shine! at 11:00 PM
Time to wind down at 7:00 AM
pass_examples: Good morning - here's how last night went
Evening check-in: winding down looks smart tonight
Afternoon update: solid day so far

criteria.description: Emoji and buddy mood coherence - mood indicator matches the overall tone of the text

scoring_guide.1: Happy emoji/mood paired with alarming or negative text
scoring_guide.2: Mood is mismatched in a noticeable way
scoring_guide.3: Mood is neutral and doesn't conflict but doesn't reinforce either
scoring_guide.4: Mood mostly matches with one minor inconsistency
scoring_guide.5: Mood perfectly reinforces the text tone

fail_examples: Cheerful buddy icon next to 'Recovery is critically low, rest immediately'
Sad mood indicator with 'Great day! You crushed your goals'
Celebratory emoji on a rest-mode warning
pass_examples: Calm buddy mood with 'Take it easy today, recovery is building'
Energetic mood with 'Strong recovery - push yourself today'
Neutral mood with 'Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.'

criteria.description: Goal motivation - goal text encourages progress, never guilts the user

scoring_guide.1: Goal text shames the user for not hitting targets
scoring_guide.2: Goal text implies disappointment or failure
scoring_guide.3: Goal text is factual but flat, no encouragement
scoring_guide.4: Goal text is encouraging with one minor guilt-adjacent phrase
scoring_guide.5: Goal text is motivating, forward-looking, and blame-free

fail_examples: You failed to hit your step goal again
Only 2,000 of 10,000 steps - not enough effort
You're being lazy, get moving
pass_examples: 3,200 steps down, 6,800 to go - a short walk gets you closer
Rest day adjusted your goal - even small movement counts
Solid start. Another 4,000 steps and you'll hit your target

criteria.description: Recovery framing - uses objective data framing, never subjective blame

scoring_guide.1: Recovery text directly blames user choices for poor recovery
scoring_guide.2: Recovery text implies user is at fault through word choice
scoring_guide.3: Recovery text is neutral but leans toward subjective framing
scoring_guide.4: Recovery text is mostly objective with one borderline phrase
scoring_guide.5: Recovery text is entirely data-driven and constructive

fail_examples: You wrecked your recovery by staying up late
Bad recovery because you didn't rest enough
Your poor choices led to low recovery
pass_examples: Recovery is lower today - sleep duration was the main factor
Prioritize rest tonight - sleep is the biggest lever for recovery.
Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.

criteria.description: Stress sensitivity - stress-related text does not cause MORE stress

scoring_guide.1: Stress text is alarming and likely to increase user anxiety
scoring_guide.2: Stress text uses urgent language that could worry the user
scoring_guide.3: Stress text is factual but cold, misses reassurance
scoring_guide.4: Stress text is calming with one slightly anxious phrase
scoring_guide.5: Stress text is calming, normalizing, and provides a gentle next step

fail_examples: WARNING: Your stress levels are dangerously high!
Your body is under extreme stress, this is concerning
Stress has been critically elevated for 3 days straight
pass_examples: Stress patterns are a bit elevated - a few deep breaths can help
Your body's been working hard. A calm evening helps reset things
Stress is higher than usual. A short walk or quiet time helps

criteria.description: Consistency across pages - dashboard message doesn't contradict stress page or insights

scoring_guide.1: Dashboard says 'great day' while stress page says 'high stress warning'
scoring_guide.3: Pages don't contradict but tell very different stories
scoring_guide.4: Pages are consistent with one minor tone mismatch
scoring_guide.5: All pages tell a coherent story with consistent tone and data

fail_examples: Dashboard: 'Excellent recovery!' / Stress: 'Your body is overwhelmed'
Insights: 'Great sleep trend' / Dashboard: 'Sleep has been poor'
Goals: 'Push hard today' / Recovery: 'Take it easy, recovery is low'
pass_examples: Dashboard: 'Solid recovery' / Stress: 'Manageable stress' / Goals: 'Moderate effort today'
Dashboard: 'Rest day' / Stress: 'Take it easy' / Goals: 'Adjusted goals for recovery'
All pages reflect improving trend with encouraging tone

criteria.description: First impression - would a non-expert understand what to do within 5 seconds?

scoring_guide.1: Text is confusing, dense, or requires domain knowledge to parse
scoring_guide.2: Text is somewhat clear but requires re-reading
scoring_guide.3: Main point is visible but action isn't immediately obvious
scoring_guide.4: Clear message and action with minor complexity
scoring_guide.5: Instantly clear what the situation is and what to do

fail_examples: Based on multi-day HRV trend analysis, parasympathetic reactivation suggests modified periodization
Composite readiness index factoring sleep, strain, and recovery yields a moderate recommendation
Your 7-day rolling SDNN average is within normal variance
pass_examples: Prioritize rest tonight - sleep is the biggest lever for recovery.
Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.
Strong day ahead - your body is ready for a solid workout
